idea-plugin.name: Spotyy
idea-plugin.description:
Spotyy — Spotify Controls + Team Music in Your IDE

Stay in the zone. Spotyy embeds Spotify playback controls directly into the JetBrains status bar and lets you see what your teammates are listening to — all without leaving your editor.

Note: Requires the Spotify desktop app to be installed and running. Playback controls work on macOS (via AppleScript) and Linux (via D-Bus / MPRIS2). The Spotify web player is not supported.

Playback Controls

  • Play / Pause — toggle playback instantly from the status bar
  • Previous & Next track — skip or rewind without leaving your IDE
  • Volume slider — click the volume icon for an inline popup slider; auto-dismisses after 5 seconds
  • Now Playing — live artist and track name in the status bar, refreshed every 3 seconds
  • Adaptive theming — icons and colours automatically match your IDE's light or dark theme
  • Zero performance impact — all Spotify communication runs on background threads

Team Music (Local Network)

  • Listening nearby — see who on your network is currently playing music and what track
  • Vibe match — get notified when you and a teammate are listening to the same song or artist
  • Focus Room — start a shared countdown timer (25 / 45 / 60 min) and invite teammates to focus together; the host can remove members at any time
  • Nearby room visibility — even if you haven't been invited, you can see that a Focus Room is running on your network, who's hosting it, how many members are in it, and how much time is left
  • Ghost mode — hide yourself from the network so no one can see what you're listening to; automatically locked while you're in a Focus Room

Getting Started

  1. Install Spotyy and restart your IDE.
  2. Open the Spotify desktop app on your Mac and start playing a track.
  3. Open any project — the Spotyy widget appears automatically in the bottom status bar.
  4. Click the 👥 or ✦ button in the status bar to open the team panel.
  5. Use the playback buttons to control music and the volume icon to adjust level.

Requirements

  • macOS 10.15 Catalina or later — or — Linux with D-Bus (any modern distro)
  • Spotify desktop application (installed and running)
  • Any JetBrains IDE: IntelliJ IDEA, Android Studio, PyCharm, WebStorm, CLion, GoLand, Rider, and more
  • All users on the same local network (office Wi-Fi, shared LAN) to see each other

Permissions

macOS: On first launch, macOS may ask for Automation permission so the IDE can control Spotify. If the widget shows no track information, open System Settings → Privacy & Security → Automation and enable Spotify access for your IDE.

Linux: Spotify is controlled via the standard D-Bus MPRIS2 interface — no extra permissions needed. Optionally install playerctl for more reliable metadata parsing (sudo apt install playerctl / sudo dnf install playerctl).

Found a bug or have a feature request? Open an issue on GitHub.
idea-plugin.change-notes:
0.0.5

  • Linux support — Spotify playback controls now work on Linux via D-Bus / MPRIS2; Spotify is auto-launched if not already running
  • First-run onboarding — OS-aware setup walkthrough shown on first launch; guides you through installing Spotify, playerctl (Linux), and granting Automation permission (macOS), with one-click copy commands
  • Light mode UI fixes — button hover color and separator are now theme-aware; no more black hover flash in light mode
  • Progress bar on theme switch — progress bar no longer disappears when switching between dark and light themes
  • Vibe match midnight reset — vibe match counter now resets at local midnight based on system clock
  • macOS: ~/Applications detection — Spotify installed in the user Applications folder is now correctly detected
  • macOS: Automation permission alert — shows a notification if the IDE is blocked from controlling Spotify, with instructions to fix it in System Settings

0.0.4

  • Nearby room visibility — non-members on the same network can now see active Focus Rooms: who's hosting, how many members are inside, and time remaining
  • Focus Room — shared countdown timer (25 / 45 / 60 min); host can invite peers and remove members at any time
  • Listening nearby — see teammates on the same network and what they're playing
  • Vibe match — highlights when you're listening to the same song or artist as a teammate
  • Ghost mode — hide yourself from the network; automatically locked while in a Focus Room
  • Broader IDE compatibility — supports IntelliJ 2022.3+ and Android Studio Giraffe and later
  • Popup layout fix — peer panel now renders at a consistent fixed width on all machines
